synthesis.notes: Electrodeposition was conducted under constant potential (chronoamperometry) at -0.5 V vs. Li or under constant current (chronopotentiometry) at 2 mA cm-2. The electrodeposition was stopped when the areal capacity reached 5 mAh cm-2. In the single-cation Li electrolyte, dendritic morphology was observed with floating deposits. The electrolyte was prepared and all experiments were conducted in a glovebox under high purity argon atmosphere. Deposits were washed with THF and dried in argon before characterization.

synthesis.notes: Na metal electrodeposition was performed from NaTFSA/G3 electrolyte at 0.5 mol L-1. The resulting deposits showed dendritic/floating morphology in the single-cation electrolyte. Na metal density (0.968 g cm-3) is lower than G3 solvent (0.986 g cm-3), causing peeled dendrites to float. Constant-current electrodeposition at 2 mA cm-2 was also performed as an alternative condition. XRD confirmed bcc Na metal phase in the deposits.

synthesis.notes: The electrodeposition potential was set to -0.5 V vs Na. The addition of CaTFSA2 modifies the solvation structure of Na+ to form contact ion pairs (CIPs) with TFSA- anions, which retards desolvation kinetics and leads to dendrite-free Na metal deposition. The electrodeposition was stopped when the areal capacity reached 5 mAh cm-2.
